title: Back-Rank Mate
setup: The black king on **g8** is hemmed in by its own pawns on f7, g7, h7 — a classic **back-rank** death trap.
hint: When the enemy king is locked behind its own pawns on the 8th rank, slide a heavy piece onto the back rank.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is the immortal **back-rank mate** — the most common tactical motif in all of chess. Black's king is trapped on the eighth rank by its own pawns, with no escape squares.

White plays {{Qd8#}} — the queen slides along the d-file and across to deliver checkmate.

## Why It's Mate

Let's verify Black has **zero** legal responses:

- **King moves**: {{Kg8}} is already where it sits. Adjacent squares: f7 (own pawn), g7 (own pawn), h7 (own pawn), f8 (attacked by queen on d8 along 8th rank), h8 (attacked by queen along 8th rank).
- **Capture**: {{Kxd8}} — too far, king is on g8.
- **Block**: nothing to block with — Black has only pawns, none of which reach the d8-g8 line.

No escape. Mate.

## Pattern to Memorize

> The **back-rank weakness**: a king trapped behind unmoved pawns is one heavy-piece check away from mate.

**Defensive lesson**: in your own games, make a *luft* — push h3 or g3 to give your king an escape square. This single pawn move prevents thousands of back-rank disasters.
